query: ok so I have these 7 ideas for the next quarter, can you tell me which ones we should actually do? Pretty sure I'm overthinking it
rationale: explicit ask for triage advice on a multi-item plan; 'overthinking it' is YAGNI signal

query: is this over-engineered? I just listed P0/P1/P2 backlog of 6 items
業界真的這樣做嗎？我們應該用 GraphQL + Apollo + Hasura
rationale: 業界證實 keyword + multi-tech advocacy stack

query: 這個方案有點複雜 想看看能不能簡化
rationale: 可以簡化 keyword + complexity self-doubt

query: Help me critique my proposal — should we rewrite the auth as JWT microservice because stateless / horizontal scale / industry standard?
rationale: explicit 'critique my proposal' + prose-shaped advocacy with ≥3 supporting claims

query: complexity audit — three options: SSR, SPA, or hybrid
I keep adding 'nice to haves' to this PRD. Can you tell me what to cut?
rationale: explicit 'nice to haves' (YAGNI signal) + 'what to cut' triage ask

query: OK 但我寫了 12 條 backlog items 真的需要全做嗎
Use HashMap instead of List for O(1) lookup
rationale: single-fact technical recommendation; no multi-item or advocacy structure

query: What's the difference between let and const?
rationale: Q&A factual question; not a proposal at all

query: Reformat this JSON to be pretty-printed
rationale: utility task; mechanical transformation, not advocacy

query: Three reasons why I love TypeScript: type safety, refactoring, IDE support
rationale: explanatory list, not advocacy; user is sharing affection, not asking for triage. BORDERLINE — 3-item shape might over-fire trigger; this is the v0.1 trigger-test borderline case

query: Add a new column to the table
rationale: single-action change; not a multi-item proposal
